backlog.title: Decide whether Control UI will ever sit behind a reverse proxy
backlog.description: If exposed through a reverse proxy later, set gateway.trustedProxies explicitly. If it stays local-only on loopback, this warning is acceptable.

notes.text: Warren: defer implementation for now, but keep Control UI proxy-compatible. It matters once the UI is internet-exposed, multi-user, or needs centralized auth/TLS/rate limiting.
Mario: defer by default unless Control UI becomes a remotely accessed product surface or shared operator tool. Revisit when public exposure, shared domain routing, or enterprise requirements appear.

backlog.title: Property Manager deferred features
backlog.description: Hold rent collection, accounting, leases, screening, owner financial reporting, vendor marketplace, and broader platform complexity until the streamlined maintenance command center proves value.

notes.text: Barry Bot: deferred feature set documented in PROPERTY_MANAGER_DEFERRED.md so the streamlined v1 can stay sharp.

inProgress.title: Property Manager
inProgress.description: Streamlined property manager app focused on maintenance request intake, status tracking, vendor dispatch, tenant updates, and property/unit issue history for small landlords.

notes.text: Barry Bot: elevated as a top active app track. Streamlined v1 concept is documented in PROPERTY_MANAGER_V1.md.
Barry Bot: formalized the streamlined v1 concept in PROPERTY_MANAGER_V1.md and split deferred heavyweight features into a separate holding task.
Mario: start as a maintenance command center for small landlords. Exclude accounting, rent, leases, screening, big owner portals, and feature gravity traps from v1.

inProgress.title: Realtor Aid
inProgress.description: Build an app for realtors that adds photo realistic staging items into listing photos for a home being listed for sale

notes.text: Barry Bot: elevated as a top active app track after Elon said this should be pursued now as the main realtor build.

Barry Bot: activated this task while the clipboard app is paused. Elon has been asked for feasibility, fastest MVP architecture, biggest risks, first prototype slice, and whether it is worth pursuing now.

inProgress.title: Youtube
inProgress.description: Create an action plan for building youtube videos. What websites do we need access to create screensavers that are 2 to 10 hours long with ambient music.

notes.text: Barry Bot: finished practical action plan written to YOUTUBE_SCREENSAVER_SOP.md. Core stack: DaVinci Resolve, FFmpeg, Canva, Pexels, Pixabay, YouTube Audio Library, Google Drive, and YouTube Studio. Includes licensing checklist, workflow, recurring cost bands, and what to avoid.
Barry Bot: kicked off Warren review for cheapest viable stack, recurring costs, licensing risks, and what to avoid.

inProgress.title: Finding app ideas
inProgress.description: Find successful apps in Windows Store that do not exist in Apple Store.

notes.text: Barry Bot: kept active as supporting research, but demoted below the current core build tracks now that it has already produced the shortlist and MVP direction.

Barry Bot: second pass complete. Top recommendation is a role-specific clipboard workflow utility. Decision pack written to TOP5_APP_OPPORTUNITIES.md with ranked Top 5 and an MVP spec for the best candidate.

Barry Bot: first concrete shortlist captured in SHORTLIST_APP_IDEAS.md. Strongest buckets: clipboard/text workflow, audio/device switching, file preview/triage, and single-purpose PowerToys-style utilities. Weak buckets: cosmetic customization and commodity archive tools.

Warren: Focus on desktop-control and workflow utilities with recurring pain. Best categories: clipboard/text workflow, file workflow, audio/device switching. Avoid cosmetic-only utilities and broad consumer apps with weak conversion.

Elon: Fastest credible path is a 48-hour sprint: scrape 200-500 Windows candidates across transferable categories, score by reviews/ratings/polish, narrow to 50-75, manually verify Apple absence, then produce a top-10 list with MVP difficulty and monetization angle.

Mario: Don’t just look for anything missing on Apple. Hunt for neglected but valuable workflows. Most interesting gaps are workflow utilities, desktop control/customization, prosumer niche tools, and light enterprise edge cases. Build a shortlist of 20, then score top 5 deeply.
Barry Bot: deduplicated three duplicate backlog cards into this single canonical task and moved it to In Progress.

inProgress.title: Realtor Drone Aid
inProgress.description: Build an app that can use the exterior listing photos to create a simulated drone photo from above. Uses google earth to get a birds eye photo as reference.

notes.text: Barry Bot: demoted to a weaker/secondary R&D track after Elon said it should not be the primary build due to accuracy and trust risk.

Barry Bot: activated this task while the clipboard app is paused. Elon has been asked for feasibility, fastest MVP architecture, biggest risks, first prototype slice, and whether it is worth pursuing now.

complete.title: App success
complete.description: Multi-store app acceptance workflow is now covered by Mission Control app workflow tracking, store-account readiness reminders, and the stage-gated process guidance returned by Mario.

notes.text: Barry Bot: marked complete because this now directly supplements the app workflow and store-readiness work already built into Mission Control.

Mario: use a multi-store readiness system with hard stage gates: strategic fit, store viability, compliance complete, submission-ready assets, and launch approval. Design for the strictest store first and standardize metadata/assets in one source of truth.
